steps.voice: The default layout. Every tile is a widget bound to a CAN signal — thirteen widget types in total, all rebindable. Long-press any one to edit it; short-tap anywhere to summon the menu.

RPM Bar up top. Horizontal fill running zero to redline, with tick marks every thousand and the redline zone in red. One per layout — that's the limit. Always across the top so it's visible even when you're glancing for a tenth of a second.

Turn-signal Indicators flank the big RPM number. Two slots per layout — left and right — bound to your blinker signals. They flash green when active. Same widget family as the warning lamps, just bigger and dedicated to the indicator stalks.

Centre column — Vehicle Speed up top, Gear right below. Both are Text widgets — plain numeric readouts, custom font and decimals per widget. Big standalone digits work best for things you read at a glance: speed, gear, peak boost.

Eight Panel widgets flank the centre — coolant, oil temp, oil pressure, lambda, intake, IAT, fuel pressure, voltage on the default layout. Each one is a coloured box with a label and a number; bind any signal, set thresholds, pick warning colours. Sixteen panels per layout is the hard cap.

Bottom row — Bar widgets. Coolant temp on the left bleeds blue to red as it climbs through the warning band; throttle on the right fills left-to-right. Two Bar widgets per layout — anything you can ramp linearly fits here, from fuel level to brake-pedal trace.

Eight Warning slots run along the very top edge — silent until their threshold trips. Oil-pressure-low, knock count, check engine, anything you've defined alerts on. Single-frame visibility instead of a number you have to read; that's the Indicator's job too in a sense, but Warnings have named labels and persist until you acknowledge or the value clears.

Long-press any widget — about half a second — to open its config modal. Three tabs: CAN Signal, Display, Alerts. Two-finger tap toggles night mode. Swipe from the left edge opens the peaks list. And a long-press on the RDM logo opens Device Settings. Three gestures, every screen.
